mission.title: Install and Configure Hyperlight on Kubernetes
mission.description: Hyperlight is a lightweight Virtual Machine Manager (VMM) designed for embedding within applications, enabling the safe execution of untrusted code in micro virtual machines with minimal overhead. Installing Hyperlight allows developers to leverage its capabilities for creating secure and efficient environments for running untrusted code.

steps.title: Add the Hyperlight Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Hyperlight Helm repository to your local Helm configuration using the following command:
```bash
helm repo add hyperlight https://charts.hyperlight.dev
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Hyperlight using Helm
steps.description:
Install Hyperlight in a new namespace called 'hyperlight' with the specified version:
```bash
helm install hyperlight hyperlight/hyperlight --namespace hyperlight --create-namespace --version v0.12.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Hyperlight pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace hyperlight
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Update the Hyperlight deployment to set resource limits for better performance:
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment hyperlight --namespace hyperlight --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi
```

steps.title: Set up port forwarding for access
steps.description:
Forward a local port to the Hyperlight service to access it:
```bash
kubectl port-forward service/hyperlight --namespace hyperlight 8080:80
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Hyperlight will show the pods in the 'hyperlight' namespace as running when you execute `kubectl get pods --namespace hyperlight`. You will also be able to access the service via port 8080 on your local machine.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add hyperlight https://charts.hyperlight.dev
helm repo update
helm install hyperlight hyperlight/hyperlight --namespace hyperlight --create-namespace --version v0.12.0
kubectl set resources deployment hyperlight --namespace hyperlight --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Hyperlight release from the Kubernetes cluster using Helm. This will remove the deployment and associated resources created by the installation.
```bash
helm uninstall hyperlight --namespace hyperlight
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volume claims (PVCs) associated with Hyperlight to ensure a clean removal.
```bash
kubectl delete crd $(kubectl get crd | grep hyperlight | awk '{print $1}')
kubectl delete pvc --all --namespace hyperlight
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'hyperlight' namespace to remove all remaining resources and verify that it has been removed successfully.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace hyperlight
kubectl get namespaces | grep hyperlight
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, create a backup of the current Hyperlight configuration and resources.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace hyperlight -o yaml > hyperlight-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade the Hyperlight release to the latest version. Ensure you specify the correct version.
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade hyperlight hyperlight/hyperlight --namespace hyperlight --version v0.12.1
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the Hyperlight pods are running the new version successfully after the upgrade.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace hyperlight
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If Hyperlight pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs to diagnose the issue.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace hyperlight
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the Hyperlight service, ensure that the port forwarding is set up correctly and check the service status.
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace hyperlight
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources
troubleshooting.description:
If pods are failing due to insufficient resources, check the resource requests and limits set for the deployment and adjust them accordingly.
```bash
kubectl describe deployment hyperlight --namespace hyperlight
```

troubleshooting.title: Helm release not found
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter an error stating that the Helm release is not found, verify that you are using the correct namespace and release name.
```bash
helm list --namespace hyperlight
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, along with Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
